content.full_text:
December 19, 1979

Mr. Terence Ryan
Treasurer, Pete Ashe for
Retirement Board
229 17th Avenue
San Francisco, California 94121

Dear Mr. Ryan:

In your telephone call to me of December 7, 1979, and your letter
of the same date you ask whether a group of people raising funds
to support a candidate for one of three elected memberships on the
Retirement Board of the San Francisco City and County Employees
Retirement System is a committee with reporting obligations under
the Political Reform Act ("the Act"). For the reasons set out
below the answer to your question is no.

Committees supporting candidates are required by the Act to file
Statement of Organization (see Government Code Section 84101)
and campaign statements (see Government Code Section 84200). A
committee is a person or group of persons receiving contributions
or making expenditures to influence the action of the voters for
or against the nomination or election of a candidate (see
Government Code Section 82013). A candidate is a person seeking
election to "any elective office" (see Government Code Section
82007).

Elective office is defined by the Act (Government Code Section
82023) to mean "any state, regional, county, municipal, district
or judicial office which is filled at an election." "Election"
in turn is specifically defined to mean "any primary, general,
special or recall election held in this state" (Government Code
Section 82022).

The Elections Code (Section 19) defines the word "election,"
used in each term of Government Code Section 82022, to mean
"any election including a primary which is provided for under
the provisions of [the Elections] code."

229--17th Avenue
San Francisco, CA 94121
December 7, 1979

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K St.
Sacramento, CA 95814

Gentlepeople:

On November 30, 1979 I filed form 410 with the Secretary of State's
Office because I was unsure whether this Committee was covered by
the Political Reform Act. I enclosed a letter asking whether we
were covered. On December 6, 1979 I received ID # 791855 but no
answer.

Mr. Ashe is running for a seat on the City's Retirement Board, a
municipal agency which oversees pension matters. Three of the
seats are elected by members of the Retirement System who only
City employees.

Are we required to file?

Are members of this Board required to file other statements, such
as statements of economic interests, etc.?

If I can provide you with any other information, please call me at
415/543-9850.

Very truly yours,

Terrence Ryan
Treasurer: Peter Ashe for
Retirement Board

tr/csa 400, seiu, afl-cio

[illegible handwritten notes at bottom]

Mr. Terence Ryan                          -2-                          December 19, 1979


According to information you have given me the San Francisco Retire-
ment Board election is provided for in the City and County Charter
under the Constitution. Therefore, it is not an "election" provided
for under the provisions of the Elections Code and so is not an
election within the meaning of Government Code Section 82022.
Therefore, the reporting and disclosure provisions of the Political
Reform Act do not apply.

In your second question you ask whether the members of the Retirement
Board are required to file Statements of Economic Interests. The
answer to this question is yes.

Every agency is required to adopt and promulgate a Conflict of
Interest Code (Government Code Section 87300) which must contain
requirements that each designated employee file annual statements
disclosing reportable investments, interests in real property and
income (see Government Code Section 87302 (b)).

"Agency" is defined by the Act to mean any State agency or local
government agency (Government Code Section 82003). A "local
Government Agency" is further defined to mean "a county, city
or district of any kind including school district, or any other
local or regional political subdivision, or any other department,
division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of
these..." (Government Code Section 82041). The Employees' Retire-
ment Board of the City and County of San Francisco is, therefore,
an agency within the meaning of the Act, and Statements of
Economic Interests are required to be filed according to the
provisions of the Act.

I hope this information has been helpful. If you require additional
assistance you may call me at 916/322-5662.

Sincerely,


JOHN GIANSELLO
Staff Service Analyst

3,670 — 3,650

funds, including the compensation allowed in the budget and salary ordinance; payrolls; and apportionment ordinances.

The commission shall have power to carry out the civil service provisions of this charter, and, except as otherwise provided in this charter, such rules shall govern applications, examinations, eligibility, classification of eligible lists, certification of eligibles, appointments, promotions, transfers, reinstating, lay-offs or reduction in force, both permanent and temporary, leaves of absence, hours and conditions of work; the filling of positions, temporary, seasonal and permanent; classification; appeals of payrolls; and such other matters as are set in rules that with this charter. The commission may, upon one week's notice, make changes in the rules, which changes shall thereupon be printed and shall have the same force and effect as if incorporated in a rule passed before the commission. The secretary may certify eligibles and payrolls and conduct examinations under the rules of the commission.

The commissioners shall have power to institute and prosecute legal procedures for violations of any of the civil service provisions of this chapter.

(b) The civil service commission shall establish an inspection service for the purpose of investigating the conduct of, and action of applicants in all positions, and of securing records of service for promotion and other purposes. All departments shall cooperate with the commission in making its investigations and any person hindering the commission or its agents shall be subject to suspension.

PART EIGHTEEN: RETIREMENT BOARD

3,670 Board Composition

The retirement system shall be managed by a retirement board, which is hereby created and which shall be the successor and have the powers and duties of the board of administration of the San Francisco employees' retirement fund, the board of the pension fund and the board of supervisors, three members to be appointed by the mayor and three members elected from the active members who shall not be retired persons of the retirement system. The board shall appoint a secretary and treasurer, and the mayor shall either hold a director of medicine or shall be appointed by the mayor from among three persons whose names shall have been submitted to him for each such appointment by a committee consisting of two members each of the San Francisco Medical Society, Bar Association of San Francisco, San Francisco Real Estate Board and the Greater San Francisco Chamber of Commerce; provided, however, that they shall not be required to hold a degree of doctor of medicine. The term of office of the six members, other than the president of the board and the members at large, and the terms presently in effect for appointed and elected members shall continue to apply. The members of the retirement board shall serve without compensation. Subject to the civil service provisions of this chapter, the retirement board shall appoint a secretary and auditor.

3,671 Functions, Powers and Duties

The retirement board shall be the sole authority and judge, under such general ordinances as may be adopted by the supervisors, as to the conditions under which members may receive, and may continue to receive, benefits under this system; and it shall have exclusive control of the administration and investment of such fund and shall be authorized and directed that all moneys of the system shall be held for insurance companies in California.

3,672 Secretary-General Manager and Auditory

The retirement board shall appoint an actuary, who shall hold office at the pleasure of the board and perform such activity. The secretary-general manager or actuary shall have the power to administer oaths and affidavits on all matters pertaining to the business of the retirement system.

PART NINETEEN: HEALTH SERVICE BOARD

3,650 Board Composition

The health service board shall consist of seven members as follows: the chairman of the finance committee of the board of supervisors, the city attorney, two members appointed by the mayor, one of whom shall be a resident official of an insurance company, and the other a doctor of medicine, and three members elected by the members of the system from among doctors and one other member herewith, an assistant city attorney in all board meetings and to act for him in his place. The terms of office of the members, other than the two ex-officio members, shall be five years, one

C

C

CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
RETIREMENT BOARD                    OFFICE OF THE GENERAL MANAGER

EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM
779 GOLDEN GATE AVENUE
SAN FRANCISCO, CA. 94102


ELECTION NOTICE

Members of the San Francisco City and County Employees' Retirement System:

PURPOSE

A term of William F. Kidd, one of the three members of the Retirement Board of the San Francisco City and County Employees' Retirement System, who has been elected a member of the Board to represent the City Employees in accordance with Charter requirement, will expire on February 20, 1980.

NOMINATIONS

Because of this expiration and to meet the Charter requirement that the membership of the Retirement Board shall include three active members of the Retirement System (which does not include pensioners), nomination of an active member of the Retirement System for membership on the Retirement Board to serve five years, will be received at the office of the Board, 779 Golden Gate Avenue, Room 260, through January 4, 1980.

All nomination must be in writing and must be signed by no less than 20 persons making the nomination. Every person nominated, as well as every person making the nomination, must be an active member of the Retirement System. Each nominee must certify in writing that they accept the nomination and if they consent to serve if elected. All nominations and election documents of the nominations must be received in the office of the Board no later than 5 p.m., January 4, 1980, and if any person nominated does not accept or consent to serve by that time, his name will not appear on the ballot.

ELECTION

On or January 22, 1980, and not later than January 31, 1980, the Registrar of Voters will cause ballots and ballot materials to be furnished to all employee members. An envelope will be addressed to each member in care of his department and will contain a ballot with instructions printed thereon and a ballot return envelope. An employee member votes by marking his ballot in accordance with instructions thereon; placing the marked ballot inside the ballot return envelope; sealing the envelope and signing his name thereon. A printed signature card will be necessary to sign the back of the ballot return envelope invalidates the ballot.

Ballots must be returned by 5 p.m., not later than 5 p.m. on February 14, 1980. Envelopes that cannot be delivered to the addressee must be returned to the Registrar's office by the department. It is suggested that completed ballots be turned to payroll clerks for prompt forwarding to the Registrar's office. Mailed ballots must be postmarked by February 14, 1980. Ballot envelopes received by mail without postage and ballots will not be accepted. Any employee who failed to receive a ballot may appear in person at the Registrar's office not later than 5 p.m., February 14, 1980 and upon execution an affidavit of non-receipt of ballot, he will be furnished a ballot and allowed to vote.

COUNTING OF VOTE

At 9 a.m., February 15, 1980, the office of the Registrar of Voters, Rm. 158, City Hall, the sealed envelopes containing the ballots will be opened in the presence of witnesses and ballots canvassed publicly in such manner that the identity of the individual casting any particular ballot will not be disclosed. The individual receiving the
